top_articles.reason: The Supersonic Aviation Modernization Act could significantly change air travel dynamics.
top_articles.title: Coast-To-Coast In 3 Hours: How The New Supersonic Bill Could Slash US Flight Times
top_articles.summary_en: The U.S. House of Representatives has passed the Supersonic Aviation Modernization (SAM) Act, which aims to lift a 50-year ban on supersonic overland flights, potentially allowing coast-to-coast travel in just three hours. With advancements in noise-mitigation technology, new supersonic jets could operate without disturbing communities below, marking a significant shift in commercial aviation.
top_articles.summary_zh: 美国众议院通过了超音速航空现代化法案（SAM法案），旨在解除对超音速陆上飞行的50年禁令，这可能使得横跨美国的旅行仅需三个小时。随着噪音减轻技术的进步，新的超音速喷气机可以在不打扰下面社区的情况下运营，标志着商业航空的重大转变。

top_articles.reason: The Air Canada crash highlights ongoing safety and recovery issues affecting passengers.
top_articles.title: Air Canada Crash Survivors Still Without Belongings Until Crews Move Aircraft To Hangar
top_articles.summary_en: Survivors of the Air Canada Express Flight 8646 crash at LaGuardia Airport are still without their personal belongings as crews work to move the wreckage to a hangar. The investigation, led by the NTSB, is ongoing, focusing on the collision's circumstances and the heroic actions of the flight crew that saved many lives.
top_articles.summary_zh: 在拉瓜迪亚机场发生的加拿大航空快运8646号航班坠毁事故的幸存者仍然无法取回他们的个人物品，因为工作人员正在努力将残骸移至机库。由国家运输安全委员会（NTSB）主导的调查仍在进行中，重点关注碰撞的情况以及拯救许多生命的机组人员的英雄行为。

top_articles.reason: Southwest's new crew base enhances operational efficiency and impacts local travel.
top_articles.title: Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew
top_articles.summary_en: Southwest Airlines has opened a new crew base at Austin-Bergstrom Airport, enhancing operational efficiency and expanding its presence in the city. This development is expected to increase the number of gates from 10 to 18, potentially doubling flight frequencies and creating over 2,000 jobs for pilots, cabin crew, and support staff.
top_articles.summary_zh: 西南航空在奥斯丁-伯格斯特罗姆机场开设了新的机组基地，提高了运营效率并扩大了在该市的业务。这一发展预计将使登机口数量从10个增加到18个，可能使航班频率翻倍，并为飞行员、机舱乘务员和支持人员创造超过2000个职位。

top_articles.reason: Hartsfield-Jackson's status as the busiest airport reflects trends in air travel.
top_articles.title: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers
top_articles.summary_en: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport remains North America's busiest airport by total passengers, processing over 106 million travelers in 2025. Its strategic geographic location and operational efficiency contribute to its dominance, despite a slight decrease in passenger volume compared to previous years.
top_articles.summary_zh: 哈茨菲尔德-杰克逊亚特兰大国际机场仍然是北美最繁忙的机场，2025年处理超过1.06亿名旅客。其战略地理位置和运营效率促成了其主导地位，尽管与前几年相比，客流量略有下降。

top_articles.reason: The potential retirement of the MiG-21 marks a significant shift in military aviation history.
top_articles.title: Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026
top_articles.summary_en: The Soviet Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-21, the most-produced fighter jet in history, is on the brink of disappearing as major operators like India have retired their last examples. While some countries may still list the MiG-21 in service, many are likely non-operational, marking the end of an era for this iconic aircraft.
top_articles.summary_zh: 苏联米高扬-古列维奇MiG-21是历史上生产数量最多的战斗机，正面临消失的边缘，因为印度等主要运营商已退役其最后的机型。虽然一些国家可能仍在列出MiG-21的服役，但许多可能已不再具备作战能力，这标志着这一标志性飞机时代的结束。

shownotes_en:
Welcome back to another episode of the Aviation News Podcast! I’m your host, Dudu, and today is March 26, 2026. We have a fantastic lineup of stories that highlight the ever-evolving world of aviation, from groundbreaking legislation to significant developments at major airports. So, let’s dive right in!

First up, we have some exciting news from the U.S. House of Representatives, which has just passed the Supersonic Aviation Modernization, or SAM, Act. This legislation aims to lift a 50-year ban on supersonic overland flights. Imagine traveling coast-to-coast in just three hours! With advancements in noise-mitigation technology, these new supersonic jets could operate without disturbing communities below. It’s a significant shift in commercial aviation that could redefine how we think about air travel.

Now, while we’re talking about aviation advancements, let’s shift gears and discuss a more somber story. Survivors of the Air Canada Express Flight 8646 crash at LaGuardia Airport are still without their personal belongings as crews work to move the wreckage to a hangar. The National Transportation Safety Board is leading the ongoing investigation, focusing on the circumstances of the collision and the heroic actions of the flight crew that saved many lives. It’s a reminder of the challenges and risks that come with flying, even as we celebrate its advancements.

On a brighter note, Southwest Airlines is making headlines with the opening of a new crew base at Austin-Bergstrom Airport. This development is set to enhance operational efficiency and significantly expand Southwest’s presence in the city. With plans to increase the number of gates from 10 to 18, we can expect to see flight frequencies potentially doubling, which is fantastic news for travelers. Plus, this expansion is projected to create over 2,000 jobs for pilots, cabin crew, and support staff. It’s a win-win for the airline and the local economy!

Speaking of bustling airports, Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport continues to hold its title as North America’s busiest airport by total passengers. Processing over 106 million travelers in 2025, its strategic geographic location and operational efficiency keep it at the forefront of air travel, even though we’ve seen a slight decrease in passenger volume compared to previous years. It’s fascinating to see how this airport remains a key player in the aviation landscape.

Finally, we turn our attention to the world of military aviation, where the iconic Soviet Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-21, the most-produced fighter jet in history, may finally be on the brink of disappearing. Major operators like India have retired their last examples, and while some countries might still list the MiG-21 as active, many are likely non-operational. This marks the end of an era for a fighter jet that has had a significant impact on aviation history.

That wraps up today’s episode! Thank you for joining me, Dudu, on this journey through the latest in aviation news. Remember to stay curious and keep flying high! Until next time, take care and safe travels!
shownotes_zh:
欢迎回到航空新闻播客！我是你的主持人Dudu，今天是2026年3月26日。我们有一系列精彩的故事，展示航空业不断发展的世界，从开创性的立法到主要机场的重要发展。让我们直接进入主题吧！

首先，我们有来自美国众议院的激动人心的消息，众议院刚刚通过了超音速航空现代化法案，简称SAM法案。这项立法旨在解除对超音速陆上飞行的50年禁令。想象一下，只需三小时就能横跨美国！随着噪音缓解技术的进步，这些新的超音速喷气机可以在不干扰地面社区的情况下运营。这是商业航空的重大转变，可能会重新定义我们对航空旅行的看法。

现在，谈到航空进步，我们来看看一个更严肃的故事。拉瓜迪亚机场的加拿大航空快运8646号航班的幸存者仍未能拿回他们的个人物品，因为工作人员正在努力将残骸移至机库。国家运输安全委员会正在进行持续的调查，重点关注碰撞的情况以及机组人员的英勇行动，拯救了许多生命。这提醒我们，尽管我们庆祝航空的进步，但飞行仍然面临挑战和风险。

另一方面，西南航空在奥斯汀-伯格斯特罗姆机场开设了新的机组基地，成为新闻焦点。这一发展将提升运营效率，并显著扩大西南航空在该市的影响力。计划将登机口数量从10个增加到18个，我们可以期待航班频率可能翻倍，这对旅客来说是个好消息。此外，这一扩张预计将为飞行员、机组人员和支持人员创造2000多个就业机会。这对航空公司和当地经济都是双赢的局面！

说到繁忙的机场，哈茨菲尔德-杰克逊亚特兰大国际机场继续保持北美最繁忙机场的称号。2025年处理超过1.06亿名旅客，其战略地理位置和运营效率使其在航空旅行的前沿，即使与前几年相比，乘客数量略有下降。看到这个机场在航空格局中仍然是关键角色，真是令人着迷。

最后，我们关注军事航空界，标志性的苏联米高扬-古列维奇MiG-21，历史上生产最多的战斗机，可能终于面临消失的边缘。印度等主要运营商已经退役了他们的最后几架，而尽管一些国家可能仍将MiG-21列为现役，但许多可能实际上已不再可用。这标志着这一在航空历史上产生重大影响的战斗机的时代结束。

今天的节目就到这里！感谢您与我Dudu一起踏上航空新闻的旅程。记得保持好奇，继续高飞！下次再见，祝您一路平安！
